full_text:
Master’s Programme in Translational Medicine (TRANSMED) 
Agreement on Master’s thesis supervision 
 
STUDENT Last name : Grönholm First name : Alva 
E-mail : alva.gronholm@h elsinki.fi Phone / mobile : 0443081481 
THESIS Language: English Scope: 30 cr 
Title: Deciphering the Functional Immune Composition and Its Clinical Impact in a Cohort of HGSC 
HR- Proficient Interval Debulking Samples 
 
 
SUPERVISION 
 The responsible supervisor must hold at least a doctoral degree. 
The supervisor and his or her unit or research group must provide the student with the necessary 
material, tools and equipment. The supervisor must allocate time for scientific supervision and provide 
feedback and advice on academic writing. If for any reason the supervisor in unable to carry out his or 
her supervisory duties and this to a significant extent extends the agreed schedule, TRANSMED director 
must be informed. 
The student is committed to working with the supervisor and completing the work within the agreed 
timetable. Responsibility for the progress of the work rests primarily with the student . 
Responsible 
supervisor 
 
 
 Name and academic degree : Anniina Färkkilä, MD PhD 
Affiliation: Research Program in Systems Oncology, Faculty of Medicine, University of Helsinki 
E-mail: anniina.farkkila@helsinki.fi 
Phone: 0505967786 
Additional 
supervisors (if

filiation: Research Program in Systems Oncology, Faculty of Medicine, University of Helsinki 
E-mail: anniina.farkkila@helsinki.fi 
Phone: 0505967786 
Additional 
supervisors (if 
any) Name and academic degree: Ada Junquera Mencía, MSc 
E-mail: ada.junquera -mencia@helsinki.fi 
Practices in 
supervision 
(guidance, 
follow -up, 
contact with 
supervisors, 
feedback on 
writin g) 
I will receive ongoing guidance and feedback from my supervisors throughout the project. 
Regular meetings will be held to review progress, discuss results, and if necessary, adjust the 
project's direction as needed to achieve the research goals.

STUDENT 
 
 Date and signature: __________________________________________________ _22.8.2024 
 
RESPONSIBLE SUPERVISOR 
 
 Date and signature: __________________________________________________ 22.8.2024 
 
ADDITIONAL SUPERVISOR(s) 
 
 Date and signature: ________________________________________________________ 22.8.2024 
 
 
 
THESIS 
PLAN ☒ 
The thesis plan is attached. 
 
The thesis plan should include 
• The title 
• Unit(s) where the experimental work will be conducted 
• Scientific background 
• Aims and objectives of the thesis 
• Thesis project description with relevant materials and methods 
• Availability of required special equipment and materials 
• Ethical considerations (necessary licenses etc.) 
• Timetable 
• References 
 
Master’s theses are public documents. A digital copy of the thesis is stored and, pending on student’s 
permission, published online. Theses for which no open publication permission has been granted, can still 
be accessed on demand. The abstract is always publishe d on line. 
 
The scope of a Master’s thesis is 30 credits, which corresponds to ca. 5 months of full -time work, including 
both research and written work. The thesis should form a coherent entity with a clear research question. 
Any materials, analyses etc. produced by others must be clearly indicated and acknowledged according to 
good scientific practice.

coherent entity with a clear research question. 
Any materials, analyses etc. produced by others must be clearly indicated and acknowledged according to 
good scientific practice. Students must follow good laboratory practices, ethical guidelines of animal 
handling and use of patient material as well as other laws and guidelines for scientific r esearch. 
 
More information on theses, including layout, submission and evaluation, is in 
https://studies.helsinki.fi/instructions/article/thesis -and-maturity -test-masters -and-licentiates -
programmes#paragraph -1288 (search degree programme with “Translational medicine”) 
 APPROVAL of plan 
 
 
____________________________________________________________ 
 TRANSMED director
